kill_fact: The load-bearing scale-out claim — a durable, defensible NVIDIA 800V design-in worth a ~$5.6B / ~100x+-forward-sales valuation — breaks on two independently verified primary facts. (1) MULTI-SOURCE, NOT SOLE-SOURCE: NVIDIA's 800V HVDC architecture is an explicit multi-vendor MGX collaboration; Infineon is named alongside Navitas working with NVIDIA on the IDENTICAL HVDC silicon/SiC/GaN socket (GlobeNewswire 2025-05-21; barchart/Yahoo MGX coverage 2026-06). It is a 'collaboration,' not a contracted, dollar-denominated, sole-source design-win — so the supposed switching-cost moat and content-per-rack durability are unproven and commoditizable. (2) FIRED OPTIONALITY FINANCED BY DILUTION: a $500M ATM was launched 2026-06-13 (UBS/Morgan Stanley/Needham, ~19.9M shares at the $25.08 pricing reference, ~3% commission) on top of a COMPLETED $122M May-2026 ATM (6,529,666 shares) — i.e. the 'fortress balance sheet' is being sold into the AI pop, with $221M cash already eroding QoQ from $237M (~$16M/qtr burn). Against this, the demonstrated business is GAAP-gross-NEGATIVE (-9.3% GM), Q1 revenue $8.6M is DOWN ~39% YoY, GAAP net loss is $33.8M/qtr, and FY2026 revenue (~$41M) is far below break-even — with the load-bearing 800V revenue still a 'end-of-year / early-next-year' (2027) promise. As a SCALE-OUT CONVICTION name the thesis fails: ~100% of the AI optionality is already in the price (ATH $34.17 on the 2026-06-03 announce day, round-tripped to ~$24), the catalyst is FIRED with a multi-quarter vacuum ahead, the demand is multi-sourceable and un-booked, and there is no fundamental support under the multiple. Default REFUTED stands — the bear independently confirmed every kill_fact against primary sources.
corrections: None of the bear's load-bearing figures required correction — all verified to the primary record: Q1-FY2026 revenue $8.6M (-39% YoY vs $14.0M), GAAP GM -9.3% (non-GAAP 39.0%), GAAP net loss $33.8M, $221M cash (down from $237M QoQ) / zero debt, $122M May ATM completed (6.53M sh) + $500M ATM launched 2026-06-13 (~9% potential dilution). Two minor framing notes: (a) the $500M ATM agents are UBS / Morgan Stanley / Needham (the May $122M ATM agents were Craig-Hallum / UBS) — the debate left the agent set unstated; (b) diluted share count is ~230M post-May-ATM (the debate's ~233-255M 'fully diluted post-both-ATMs' is forward-looking and contingent on the open $500M ATM being drawn — directionally correct, not yet realized).
evidence: GlobeNewswire, 'NVIDIA Selects Navitas to Collaborate on Next Generation 800 V HVDC Architecture,' 2025-05-21 — establishes the relationship as a collaboration on a reference architecture, not a sole-source supply contract (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/05/21/3086170/0/en/nvidia-selects-navitas-to-collaborate-on-next-generation-800-v-hvdc-architecture.html).

Barchart / Yahoo Finance, 'Navitas Collaborates with NVIDIA MGX Ecosystem to Accelerate 800 VDC AI Infrastructure,' 2026-06 — confirms MGX is a multi-vendor ecosystem and that Infineon is also working with NVIDIA on the same 800V HVDC silicon/SiC/GaN socket; Navitas attended the NVIDIA Partner Ceremony 2026-05-29 (Taipei).

StockTitan / MarketScreener SEC-filing coverage of Navitas Form S-3ASR + 424B5 prospectus supplement, 'Navitas launches $500M ATM for Class A shares,' filed 2026-06-13 — agents UBS / Morgan Stanley / Needham, up to 3.0% commission, $25.08 reference price (06-05) implying ~19,936,204 shares; stock -6.7% on the news; director Ranbir Singh resigned same day.

Navitas Q1-FY2026 results (reported 2026-05-06; Form 10-Q period ended 2026-03-31) via StockTitan / Compound Semiconductor — revenue $8.6M (-39% YoY vs $14.0M, +18% QoQ), GAAP gross margin -9.3% (non-GAAP 39.0%), GAAP net loss $33.8M (-$0.15 EPS), cash $221M (down from $237M QoQ), zero debt; plus the completed $122M May-2026 ATM (8-K, 6,529,666 shares).
